key_items: Bitcoin most oversold since 2020 crash with RSI mirrors suggesting 30-50% rebound potential to $70K
Bitcoin bears face $2.6B short squeeze trap as funding rates drop from recent capitulation
Schwab strategist identifies $60K mining cost as potential cycle bottom with technical CVDD data suggesting reversal
reasoning: Bitcoin has crashed 50% from peaks and now trades near $60K mining cost support with extreme oversold conditions (RSI comparable to 2020/2026 reversals) and massive short positioning creating squeeze dynamics. However, macro headwinds persist including AI capital rotation, stronger-than-expected jobs data pressuring rates, BlackRock selling, and regulatory uncertainty, limiting upside momentum despite technical bounce setup.
risks: Continued capital rotation into AI, higher interest rates from stronger employment data, and potential regulatory crackdowns on crypto exchanges and banking access could extend the downside below $60K support despite oversold technicals.
